item.title: Marcoleta blasts Lacson anew over 'delayed' flood control report
item.description: In another installment of their word war, Senator Rodante Marcoleta blasted Senate President Pro Tempore and Senate Blue Ribbon Committee Chair Panfilo Lacson anew, this time due to the inability to issue a committee report on the flood control scandal seven months after the investigation began.

item.title: De Lima calls out Sara Duterte’s silence amid 2 lawmakers’ 'misogynistic' remarks
item.description: House Senior Deputy Minority Leader and ML Party List Representative Leila de Lima called out Vice President Sara Duterte’s supposed “silence” on the remarks issued by two lawmakers, which are seen as “misogynistic” and “disrespectful” of women.

item.title: PNP monitors calls for motorcade rally on Duterte arrest anniversary
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) said it is monitoring online posts calling for a massive motorcade rally on March 11, marking one year since the arrest and transfer of former president Rodrigo Duterte to international custody in The Hague.

item.title: Michelle Dee files perjury complaint vs driver
item.description: Beauty queen and actress Michelle Dee filed Monday a perjury case against the driver who accused her of illegally detaining and torturing him, in the latest development in the legal battle between the two parties.

item.title: DOJ implements 4-day on-site work week
item.description: Justice Secretary Fredderick Vida has directed officials and personnel of the Department of Justice to strictly implement a four-day on-site work week, as part of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s call for energy conservation efforts amid rising oil prices due to tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
